title: Zara and the Missing Bananas
synopsis: When all the bananas disappear from the zoo, young zookeeper Zara teams up with her animal friends to solve the mystery and discover the surprising culprit behind the great banana heist.

pages.text: Zara loved working at the Happy Valley Zoo. She had curly black hair and always wore her favorite purple hat. Every morning, she fed all the animals. Today something was very wrong. "Oh no!" said Zara. "Where are all the bananas?"

illustration.description: A young girl with curly black hair wearing a purple hat and green zoo uniform stands shocked in a zoo kitchen. Empty banana bins are scattered around. Bright morning sunlight streams through windows. Her expression shows surprise with wide eyes and an open mouth.
illustration.alt: Zara discovers empty banana bins in the zoo kitchen

pages.text: The monkeys were very upset. "We want our bananas!" chattered Maya the monkey. She had golden fur and kind brown eyes. "Someone took them all!" The elephants trumpeted sadly. Even the parrots were squawking loudly. "This is a mystery," said Zara. "I need to find those bananas!"

illustration.description: A golden-furred monkey with expressive brown eyes sits on a tree branch looking sad and frustrated. In the background, gray elephants with their trunks raised and colorful parrots are making noise. The zoo setting has lush green trees and bright blue sky.
illustration.alt: Maya the monkey and other animals complain about missing bananas

pages.text: Zara put on her detective hat. It was blue with a big magnifying glass pin. "Don't worry, friends. Detective Zara is on the case!" She looked for clues everywhere. There were no banana peels on the ground. There were no broken locks. "This is very strange," she said.

illustration.description: Zara wearing both her purple hat and a blue detective hat, holding a magnifying glass. She's examining the ground near the animal enclosures with a determined expression. Various diverse zoo visitors watch curiously in the background. Sunny day with vibrant colors.
illustration.alt: Detective Zara searches for clues with her magnifying glass

pages.text: Zara found her first clue! There were tiny footprints near the banana storage. "These are very small," she said. Maya the monkey jumped down to help. "Those are not monkey prints," said Maya. "They are too little!" The footprints led toward the bird area.

illustration.description: Zara and Maya the monkey crouch down examining tiny footprints in the dirt. Zara points at the small tracks with excitement while Maya scratches her head thoughtfully. The prints lead toward colorful bird enclosures in the distance.
illustration.alt: Zara and Maya examine tiny footprints leading to the bird area

pages.text: At the bird area, they met Carlos the parrot. He had bright red and blue feathers. "Did you see anyone take the bananas?" asked Zara. Carlos flapped his wings. "I saw something moving very early this morning. It was small and brown!" "Which way did it go?" asked Maya.

illustration.description: A magnificent parrot with bright red and blue feathers perched on a wooden post, gesturing with one wing. Zara and Maya listen intently. The tropical bird habitat has vibrant green plants and colorful flowers. Carlos has large expressive eyes and appears helpful.
illustration.alt: Carlos the parrot gives Zara and Maya important information

pages.text: "Follow me!" squawked Carlos. They walked past the giraffes and zebras. They walked past the lions sleeping in the sun. The footprints got clearer and clearer. "We're getting close!" said Zara. Her heart was beating fast with excitement. What would they find?

illustration.description: Zara, Maya, and Carlos walking past various zoo animals in a colorful parade. Tall spotted giraffes, black and white striped zebras, and golden lions lounging in sunshine. The trio follows a trail with determined expressions, moving through the lush zoo landscape.
illustration.alt: The detective team walks past various zoo animals following the trail

pages.text: The footprints led to a big oak tree. Zara looked up high in the branches. "I see something yellow!" she called. There were bananas everywhere in the tree! But who put them there? Maya climbed up to look. "Come see this!" she called down.

illustration.description: A massive oak tree with thick branches filled with bright yellow bananas hanging like decorations. Zara points upward with amazement while Maya climbs the tree trunk. Dappled sunlight filters through green leaves creating a magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Bananas discovered hanging in a large oak tree

pages.text: In the tree was a family of squirrels! There was Papa Squirrel, Mama Squirrel, and three baby squirrels. They all had fluffy brown tails and worried faces. "Please don't be angry!" said Papa Squirrel. "We were just trying to help!" said Mama Squirrel softly. The baby squirrels hid behind their parents.

illustration.description: A family of adorable brown squirrels with big fluffy tails and enormous expressive eyes looking worried and apologetic. They huddle together on a tree branch surrounded by bananas. The baby squirrels peek out shyly from behind their parents. Warm golden lighting.
illustration.alt: A family of squirrels looking worried in the banana-filled tree

pages.text: "Help with what?" asked Zara kindly. She was not angry at all. Papa Squirrel explained, "We heard there was going to be a big storm. We wanted to keep the bananas safe and dry!" "We worked all night to move them," added Mama Squirrel. "We wanted to surprise everyone!"

illustration.description: The squirrel family looks relieved as Papa Squirrel gestures while explaining their plan. Zara smiles warmly up at them with understanding. Maya and Carlos listen with interest. The scene shows the family becoming less worried and more hopeful.
illustration.alt: Papa Squirrel explains their helpful plan to surprised Zara

pages.text: "That was very thoughtful!" said Zara. "But the weather forecast shows sunny skies all week." The squirrels looked embarrassed. "Oops!" giggled the baby squirrels. "We made a mistake!" Everyone started laughing. Even Carlos was chuckling and flapping his wings.

illustration.description: Everyone bursting into joyful laughter - Zara with her head tilted back, Maya covering her mouth while giggling, Carlos flapping wings with amusement, and the squirrel family laughing together. Bright, cheerful atmosphere with warm sunlight filtering through leaves.
illustration.alt: Everyone laughing together about the squirrels' silly mistake

pages.text: Working together, they moved all the bananas back to the zoo kitchen. The squirrels were very fast climbers! Maya helped carry the big bunches. Carlos flew ahead to tell the other animals the good news. "Teamwork makes everything better!" said Zara happily.

illustration.description: A joyful scene of everyone working together - squirrels scampering up and down with bananas, Maya carrying large bunches, Zara organizing the transport, and Carlos flying overhead. Busy, cooperative atmosphere with lots of yellow bananas being moved efficiently.
illustration.alt: The team working together to return all the bananas

pages.text: Soon all the animals had their breakfast. The monkeys were swinging happily. The elephants trumpeted with joy. Even the squirrel family got some banana pieces as a thank-you treat. "Mystery solved!" said Detective Zara. "And we made new friends too!" Everyone cheered loudly. It was the best day ever at Happy Valley Zoo.

illustration.description: A celebration scene at the zoo with all the animals happy and eating. Monkeys swing joyfully, elephants raise their trunks in celebration, colorful parrots fly around, and the squirrel family enjoys banana treats. Zara stands proudly in the center with her detective hat, surrounded by diverse, cheerful zoo visitors.
illustration.alt: All the zoo animals celebrating and enjoying their bananas together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from the zoo?
questions.explanation: All the bananas disappeared from the zoo, making the animals very upset, especially the monkeys!

questions.question: Who helped Zara solve the mystery?
choices: Maya the monkey and Carlos the parrot
questions.explanation: Maya the monkey and Carlos the parrot were Zara's main helpers in solving the banana mystery.

questions.question: Where did they find the bananas?
questions.explanation: The bananas were hanging in a big oak tree where the squirrel family had moved them.

questions.question: Why did the squirrels take the bananas?
choices: To keep them safe from a storm
questions.explanation: The squirrels thought there would be a big storm and wanted to keep the bananas safe and dry.

questions.question: How did the story end?
choices: Everyone worked together and became friends
questions.explanation: Everyone worked together to return the bananas, and the squirrel family became new friends at the zoo.

pages.text: Help Zara solve the mystery! Fill in the missing words: Zara found tiny _______ near the banana storage. Carlos the parrot saw something small and _______ moving early in the morning. The _______ family was trying to help by keeping the bananas safe from a storm.

Detective Zara's case notes! Complete the sentences: The mystery started when all the _______ went missing. Zara wore her special _______ hat while looking for clues. Working as a _______ helped everyone solve the problem and make new friends.
